user_query:
Act as our internal security compliance officer. Read the policy in input/policies/production.json, the three sample components in input/skills/*.json, and the approved exemptions in input/exemptions.json. Assess each component against the production policy using only the data in those JSON files (do not call external tools). Apply these expectations:
- Treat the rules exactly as defined in input/policies/production.json.
- For scanner severity rules, fail if any finding at or above the rule’s threshold exists beyond the allowed count.
- For trust level, pass only if the level is at least TRUSTED (TRUSTED or VERIFIED).
- For pattern-absence rules, check the provided code text for any of the rule’s patterns (case-insensitive); if any pattern appears, the rule fails.
- For checksums, use the provided checksums flag in the component JSON.
- For version pinning, all dependency lines must include either "==" or ">=".
- Apply exemptions from input/exemptions.json: a rule that fails but has an approved exemption for that component should be marked exempted and should not count as a failure; if all failures are exempted and the rest pass, overall status is EXEMPTED. If all rules pass, status is COMPLIANT. Otherwise, NON-COMPLIANT.

Write the following files under output/:
1) output/assessments/acme-budget-tracker__production.json
2) output/assessments/net-monitor__production.json
3) output/assessments/calc-engine__production.json
Each assessment must be valid JSON with fields: skill (component name), policy ("production"), status (COMPLIANT | NON-COMPLIANT | EXEMPTED), assessed_at (ISO 8601), rules_total, rules_passed, rules_failed, rules_exempted, and results (array of per-rule entries with: rule, severity, frameworks, passed (bool), exempted (bool), details object).

4) output/report.json — an aggregate JSON report containing: policy ("production"), policy_description, generated_at (ISO 8601), total_skills, compliant, non_compliant, exempted, compliance_rate (percentage as number), framework_violations (map of framework name → list of {skill, rule, severity}), and skills (array of the three assessment objects you generated).

5) output/summary.txt — a human-readable summary with one line per component in the form: "<name>: <status> — passed <n>/<total> (exempted <e>, failed <f>)".

6) output/remediations.md — for every non-exempt failing rule across all components, propose a clear remediation action plan (one section per component with bullets per failing rule, including rationale, recommended changes, and an owner/ETA placeholder).
